query: What does shareReplay do in RxJS and why break Angular apps?

selection.greatFrontendReasonIfOmitted: No exact-topic GreatFrontEnd page was confirmed for this Angular, RxJS, or NgRx prompt, so broader Angular question-bank pages would be weaker competitors than topic-direct sources.

competitors.label: RxJS shareReplay
competitors.selectionReason: The official shareReplay operator docs are the canonical source for what the operator does and how replayed shared subscriptions behave.
theirStrengths: It is the official operator reference for shareReplay semantics, parameters, and replay behavior.
theirGaps: It is operator-first and less Angular-shaped around stale cache bugs and service misuse.

realWorldUseCases:
Cache-and-share operator

shareReplay does two things at once: it shares one subscription across subscribers and it replays the last emitted values to late subscribers. That is perfect for caching a cold HTTP request, but dangerous for long-lived streams when stale cached values or never-reset subscriptions stick around longer than you expected.
followUpConfusion:
Rule of thumb
  • Cold HTTP caching is the safe default use case.
  • Long-lived app-state streams need explicit thinking about refCount, reset behavior, and stale data.
  • If you cannot explain when the cached value disappears, you have not finished the operator design.

realWorldUseCases: The competitor covers the core concept, but our page is now more explicit about the production decision or failure mode behind this topic.
actionableExamples: The competitor still provides competitive examples, so the example bar remains tied instead of a clean sweep.

competitors.label: Learn RxJS shareReplay
competitors.selectionReason: Learn RxJS is the strongest tutorial-style competitor for shareReplay because it explains sharing side effects and replaying values in a practical way.
theirStrengths: It explains why shareReplay is useful when you want to avoid repeated side effects and support late subscribers.
theirGaps: It is general RxJS guidance and lighter on Angular service cache pitfalls.

competitors.label: Angular HTTP Client - QuickStart Guide
competitors.selectionReason: This Angular University guide is a topic-direct Angular competitor because it uses shareReplay in HTTP flows to prevent duplicate requests from multiple subscribers.
theirStrengths: It is Angular-specific and directly shows shareReplay in an HTTP use case.
theirGaps: It is broader on Angular HTTP and does not center the operator around stale data and long-lived cache bugs.
